output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your rights, valid deductions, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds—no signup needed.
content_outline: Introduction: The Frustrating Reality of Withheld Security Deposits
Is Your Landlord Allowed to Keep Your Deposit? Know Your Tenant Rights
What Landlords Can (and Cannot) Deduct From Your Security Deposit
State-by-State Timelines: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
5 Steps to Take When Your Landlord Won’t Return Your Deposit
How a Security Deposit Demand Letter Forces Action (And How to Write One in 60 Seconds)
When to Escalate: Small Claims Court and Penalties
key_points: POV: You moved out months ago and your landlord is still 'processing' your deposit. Here's what they don't want you to know.
3 things your landlord legally cannot deduct from your security deposit—number 2 will shock you.
Your landlord only has 14 to 30 days to return your deposit depending on your state. Miss that deadline? They could owe you double.
Stop sending angry texts. This 60-second demand letter got my $1,800 back in 48 hours with zero lawyers.
Normal wear and tear is not damage. If they charged you for faded paint or carpet wear, fight back.
For $19.99 and no signup, you can send a legally sound demand letter that puts the law on your side today.
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup or subscription required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposits and need a fast, affordable way to demand their money back.
